content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of size NNN and an integer XXX.

Find the count of all the pairs (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) such that ((Ai⊕Aj)((A_i  \oplus  A_j)((Ai​⊕Aj​) &\&& X)=0X) = 0X)=0. Here ⊕\oplus⊕ and &\&& denote bitwise XOR and bitwise AND operations respectively..


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ - the elements of the array.
The third line of each test case contains a single integer XXX.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ― the total number of pairs satisfying the condition.
content.inputFormat:
The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case:
- The first line contains an integer N, the size of the array.
- The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
- The third line contains an integer X.
All indices are 1‑based.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of ordered pairs (i, j) (1 ≤ i, j ≤ N) such that ((Ai XOR Aj) & X) = 0.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For the first test case X=1, the values of Ai & X are [1,0,1,1]; frequencies are {0:1,1:3}. Answer = 1^2 + 3^2 = 10. For the second test case X=21, all Ai & X are 0, frequency 3, answer = 3^2 = 9.

solution.approach: For a pair (i, j) the condition ((Ai XOR Aj) & X) = 0 means that on every bit where X has a 1, Ai and Aj must have the same bit. Therefore Ai & X must be equal to Aj & X. Define key = Ai & X. All elements with the same key form a group; any ordered pair inside the group satisfies the condition. If a group contains c elements it contributes c·c pairs (including i = j). The answer is the sum of c² over all groups. We compute the frequencies of each key using a hash map (or by sorting) in O(N) time per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case (hash‑map insertions and look‑ups are O(1) on average).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(K) where K is the number of distinct keys (K ≤ N).
